foundation.title: LOOP
foundation.subtitle: For Loops
foundation.content: A C++ 'for' loop is flexible. It allows you to initialize, test, and update with every repeat. Standard for array-traversal.
foundation.scenario: A 100-Meter Dash. Before starting, initialize a count (int i=0), check for the goal (i < 100), and take a step (i++).

examples.title: Standard Loop
examples.explanation: The basic 3-part loop used in almost every C++ engine.

examples.title: Modern For
examples.explanation: Go through every item in a container like a 'vector' or 'array' instantly.

examples.title: Reverse Iter
examples.explanation: Starting at 10 and counting down precisely to zero.
examples.code: for (int i=10; i>=0; i--) { cout << i << endl; }

foundation.title: WHILE
foundation.subtitle: Condition Loops
foundation.content: A C++ 'while' loop repeats as long as its condition remains True. It is evaluated at the top of every cycle.
foundation.scenario: A Waiter waiting for a customer to finish. While (is_eating is true), the waiter stands there. He can't leave until the bowl is empty (False).

examples.title: Top-Down Test
examples.explanation: Checking the switch before every single repeat of the code.

examples.title: Wait for Input
examples.explanation: Halting the program loop until a specific keyboard char like 'q' is hit.

examples.title: Do-While
examples.explanation: A special loop that runs at least once, then checks the rule at the bottom.

foundation.keyPoint: while loop checks BEFORE running. do-while checks AFTER.

foundation.title: MATH
foundation.subtitle: Arithmetic Library
foundation.content: C++ provides <cmath>. It's incredibly fast at math and used for physics-heavy games and simulations.
foundation.scenario: A Rocket Launching Calculation. Calculating the thrust (+), the drag (-), the speed (*), and the fuel weight (/).

examples.title: Basic Calc
examples.explanation: Standard math (+, -, *, /) using memory-efficient types (float, double).

examples.title: The Power Op
examples.explanation: Using the pow() function for complex scientific powers.

examples.title: The Modulo %
examples.explanation: Finding the remainder of integer division. Useful for screen wrapping logic.

gameData.q: Which Java loop runs at least once?
